bugfix-uat-flag.description: Extends the bug-fix skill to accept --uat flag. When provided, reads UAT-FAILURES.md for the specified set and converts failures into bug investigation targets.

uat-failures-format.description: Structured UAT failure artifact with embedded JSON metadata block (<!-- UAT-FAILURES-META {...} -->) containing an array of failure objects with fields: id, criterion, step, description, severity, relevantFiles.

reads-not-modifies-failures.description: The --uat flag reads UAT-FAILURES.md but never modifies it. The original failure record is preserved.

falls-back-to-interactive.description: When invoked without --uat, bug-fix behaves identically to its current implementation (backward compatible).

definition.scope: Extend /rapid:bug-fix to accept --uat argument that reads UAT-FAILURES.md and fixes reported issues through the existing executor pipeline.

tasks.description: Add --uat flag parsing to bug-fix skill
tasks.acceptance: Flag is parsed from user input and set-id is resolved

tasks.description: Implement UAT-FAILURES.md reader in bug-fix skill
tasks.acceptance: Failures are read and converted to investigation targets

tasks.description: Wire failure targets into existing executor dispatch pipeline
tasks.acceptance: Each failure produces a fix attempt via rapid-executor

tasks.description: Unit tests for --uat flag and failure file reading
tasks.acceptance: Tests cover flag parsing, file reading, and backward compatibility

acceptance: --uat flag reads UAT-FAILURES.md and dispatches fixes
Without --uat, bug-fix works identically to current behavior
CONTRACT.json imports from uat-workflow are satisfied
